summary: Matched or improved ground-stage code for Pokémon Stadium and moved F-Zero car code closer to matching. The PR renamed two Stadium render/display callbacks from anonymous fn_* symbols to grStadium_* names, updated symbols and headers, adjusted Stadium control flow around image-desc wrapper initialization and a dont-inline workaround, and refactored F-Zero car JObj setup to use typed DynamicModelDesc access, GET_JOBJ, direct table indexing, and a small scale helper.
what_changed: Renamed Stadium functions at 0x801D2FD0 and 0x801D3084 from fn_801D2FD0/fn_801D3084 to grStadium_801D2FD0/grStadium_801D3084 in symbols.txt, grpstadium.c, and grpstadium.h.
Updated grStadium_801D2D78 and grStadium_801D2E24 to pass the renamed grStadium_* callbacks to GObj_SetupGXLinkMax.

Reworked grStadium_801D2FD0 so the early-return pattern became an if (!wrapper->flag) block, introduced a local copy pointer, used decimal 36 for the lb_800122C8 size argument, and kept wrapper flagging plus PsType_Display lookup inside that block.
Replaced a custom inline grStadium_801D42B8_dontinline wrapper with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around grStadium_801D42B8, then called grStadium_801D42B8 directly from grStadium_801D4548.
In grfzerocar.c, added includes for sc/types.h and baselib/gobj.h, likely to support typed archive/model data and GET_JOBJ usage.
Changed setup_car_child to interpret archive model data as DynamicModelDesc instead of manually loading pointers from byte offsets, using entry->anims and entry->matanims.
Introduced static inline multiplyScale(HSD_JObj*, f32) to centralize the GetScale/multiply/SetScale sequence.
Changed grFZeroCar_801CAFBC to use GET_JOBJ(gobj), remove some temporaries and PAD_STACK, index data_ptr and grFZeroCar_803E0BD8 directly by i, and compute setup_car_child arguments at call sites.
smart_moves: Replacing raw pointer arithmetic in setup_car_child with DynamicModelDesc fields made archive-entry interpretation clearer while preserving the same animation and material-animation checks.
Extracting the repeated scale multiplication sequence into multiplyScale kept the call site smaller and may have helped match instruction/register pressure.
Using #pragma dont_inline on the target function itself avoided maintaining a separate dontinline trampoline solely to influence code generation.
Renaming fn_* Stadium callbacks and updating config symbols, C definitions, call sites, and header prototypes together kept symbol metadata consistent with source.
decomp_lessons: When a function is no longer anonymous, update symbols.txt, the C definition, all call sites, and the header prototype in the same PR to avoid drift.
Typed struct access such as DynamicModelDesc can replace byte-offset loads when the data layout is understood, improving readability without necessarily hurting matching.
Small helper inlines can be useful matching tools for isolating repeated JObj manipulation patterns, especially when register allocation or stack layout is sensitive.
A compiler pragma around a real function can be preferable to an artificial wrapper when the goal is only to prevent inlining for matching.
Direct array indexing can sometimes match better than carrying incremented table pointers, even when the source logic is equivalent.
naming_conventions: Stage-local matched functions use the stage prefix plus address form, e.g. grStadium_801D2FD0 and grStadium_801D3084.
Still-unknown functions may remain as fn_<address>, as shown by fn_801D2ED0 remaining unchanged.
Static inline helpers in this slice used descriptive camelCase naming, e.g. multiplyScale.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed PAD_STACK(0x18) from grFZeroCar_801CAFBC after refactoring locals, suggesting stack shaping improved enough that the padding was no longer needed.
Replaced gobj->hsd_obj with GET_JOBJ(gobj), using project macros to match expected object access style.
Changed table iteration from an incremented grFZeroCarEntry* to grFZeroCar_803E0BD8[i] direct indexing.
Moved active initialization before child checks and adjusted local ordering in grStadium_801D2FD0, likely to affect generated control flow and register allocation.
Used #pragma dont_inline on grStadium_801D42B8 instead of an inline wrapper to preserve call behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match grfzerocar.c or grFZeroCar_801CAFBC after this closer-to-match refactor.
Search for additional fn_801D* symbols in grpstadium.c that were later renamed to grStadium_*.
Look for other PRs replacing manual archive byte offsets with DynamicModelDesc field access.
Check whether #pragma dont_inline around grStadium_801D42B8 remained or was later removed after matching improvements.
